channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Trump taps Keith Kellogg as special envoy to Ukraine and Russia
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced his appointment of Keith Kellogg, a retired lieutenant general who served as chief of staff on Trump’s National Security Council during his first term, as special envoy to Ukraine and Russia.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: NATO chief: Ukraine not in strong position to negotiate with Putin
item.description: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said on Wednesday that Ukraine is not in a strong enough position to begin negotiating an end to the war with Russia and said any deal struck must “prevent the Russian from getting what they want.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire takes effect with region on edge
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah took effect early Wednesday morning, after the Israeli and Lebanese governments approved a U.S. proposal to halt the fighting in a truce that President Biden called a permanent cessation of hostilities. Ahead of the ceasefire deadline, 4 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: The Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire quiets one front but Gaza sees no end to war
item.description: For many across the Middle East, the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire came as a relief: the first major sign of progress in the region since war began more than a year ago.
item.dc:creator: Julia Frankel, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A new chancellor is elected for Oxford University's 800-year-old post
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Former U.K. Conservative Party leader William Hague has been elected chancellor of Oxford University, one of the most prestigious positions in British academia, the university said Wednesday. Hague was elected in an online vote by staff and alumni of the 800-year-old university.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biggest November snowstorm in half century hits Seoul and grounds flights
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The biggest November snowstorm to hit South Korea’s capital in more than a half century blanketed the capital on Wednesday, grounding hundreds of flights, disrupting commuter traffic and leaving at least two dead.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Philippine police file criminal complaints against Sara Duterte and her security aides
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine police officials on Wednesday filed criminal complaints against Vice President Sara Duterte and her security staff for allegedly assaulting authorities and disobeying orders in a recent altercation in Congress.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know about the ceasefire deal between Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah
item.description: A ceasefire deal that went into effect on Wednesday could end more than a year of cross-border fighting between Israel and Lebanon's Hezbollah militant group, raising hopes and renewing difficult questions in a region gripped by conflict. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: ADAM GELLER, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Namibia votes and could have its first female leader. But election upsets have shaken the region
item.description: A woman who joined Namibia’s independence movement in the 1970s is a strong contender to become its first female leader as the country votes in a presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Middle East latest: Displaced people return to south Lebanon as ceasefire appears to hold
item.description: Long-displaced residents of south Lebanon have started returning to their homes after a ceasefire between Israel and the Hezbollah militant group took effect early Wednesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Middle East latest: Ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon begins
item.description: A ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon’s Hezbollah militants began early Wednesday morning, after Beirut residents endured the most intense day of Israeli strikes since the war began. Many wondered if the agreement to stop fighting would hold.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australia's plan to ban children from social media proves popular and problematic
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — How do you protect children from the harms of social media? Politically, the answer appears simple in Australia, as legislation tackling the problem moves quickly through the Parliament. Practically, however, the solution could be far more difficult.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Texas Democrat says Trump's tariffs 'will definitely get Mexico to the table' to solve immigration, fentanyl problems
item.description: Rep. Henry Cuellar (D-Texas) said Tuesday that President-elect Trump’s tariffs “will definitely get Mexico to the table” to solve immigration and fentanyl problems.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: G7 ministers throw support behind Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire but make no mention of ICC warrant
item.description: FIUGGI, Italy (AP) — Foreign ministers from leading industrialized countries threw their strong support Tuesday behind an immediate ceasefire between Israel and the Lebanese militant group Hezbollah but sidestepped the question of whether to enforce an international arrest warrant for Israel’s leade
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Philippine investigators summon VP Duterte over her public threats against President Marcos
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine authorities handed a subpoena to Vice President Sara Duterte’s office Tuesday, inviting her to answer investigators' questions after she publicly threatened to have the president, his wife and the House of Representatives speaker assassinated if she were killed
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Childhood cancer patients in Lebanon must battle disease while under fire
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Carol Zeghayer gripped her IV as she hurried down the brightly lit hallway of Beirut’s children’s cancer center. The 9-year-old's face brightened when she spotted her playmates from the oncology ward.
item.dc:creator: SALLY ABOU AL JOUD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pakistani authorities start clearing Imran Khan supporters from the capital after deadly clashes
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Pakistani security forces launched an operation Tuesday night to disperse supporters of imprisoned former Prime Minister Imran Khan who had gathered in the capital to demand his release from prison.
item.dc:creator: RIAZAT BUTT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden team briefed Trump transition on Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire
item.description: President-elect Trump's transition team has received briefings from the chief architect of the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire deal under President Biden, a senior administration official said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Unidentified drones spotted over US military sites in UK for second week
item.description: The Pentagon is “actively” watching for drones over U.S. Air Force locations in the United Kingdom, following a series of unexplained sightings over the past two weeks. The sightings, which took place over three U.S. air bases last week between Nov.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Biden to make new push for ceasefire in Gaza after Israel-Hezbollah deal
item.description: President Biden on Tuesday said the U.S. will make a new push for a ceasefire in Gaza in the coming days, after Israel and Hezbollah agreed to pause their fighting in and around the border with Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, The Hill

item.title: Russian migrants being denied access to asylum process: attorney
item.description: "They are being subjected to a blanket policy that they are applying to Russian asylum seekers," he said.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, NewsNation

item.title: New subway system in Greece puts archaeological finds discovered during construction on display for passengers
item.description: A new subway system is on its way to being showcased to passengers in Thessaloniki, Greece. The project unveiled thousands of ancient finds through excavations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden announces cease-fire plan between Israel and Hezbollah ending 14 months of fighting
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu announced his support for a cease-fire deal that has yet to be finalized between Israel and Hezbollah terrorists.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russian forces capture former British soldier fighting for Ukraine in Kursk: report
item.description: A former British Army soldier was reportedly captured by Russian forces while fighting alongside Ukrainian soldiers in the Kursk region of the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bah, humbug! Vandal smashes Ebenezer Scrooge's tombstone used in 'A Christmas Carol' movie
item.description: If life imitates art, a vandal may be haunted by The Ghost of Christmas Yet to Come for smashing a tombstone that marked the fictional grave of Ebenezer Scrooge in the English countryside.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Uruguay ousts conservative government, elects leftist opposition candidate as turnout hits 90 percent
item.description: Leftist opposition candidate Yamandú Orsi claims victory in Uruguay's run-off presidential election, defeating center-right candidate Álvaro Delgado.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hezbollah fires barrage of rockets into Israel after IDF targets Hezbollah command centers in Beirut
item.description: Hezbollah on Sunday fired missiles and other projectiles into Israel in retaliation for to deadly IDF strikes on the militant group’s command centers in Beirut.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Catherine Herridge wants Trump 'sit-down' on bipartisan press bill
item.description: “This case involves the First Amendment and the right of reporters in the future,” Catherine Herridge said.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Death toll in Gaza from Israel-Hamas war passes 44,000, Palestinian officials say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The death toll in the Gaza Strip from the 13-month-old war between Israel and Hamas has surpassed 44,000, local health officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and FATMA KHALED, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Ukraine says Russia launched an intercontinental missile in an attack for the first time in the war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said a missile used “matches the speed and altitude” of an ICBM. The Kremlin refused to comment on the strike.
item.dc:creator: Hanna Arhirova, WGN 9

item.title: Admiral: Middle East, Ukraine conflicts strain US air defense stocks
item.description: Conflicts in the Middle East and Ukraine are adding a strain to the U.S. military’s ability to respond to China, a top U.S. admiral warns.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation
